findings.description: No rules were extracted at all for this blob despite it containing at least four distinct, independently governed rules: (1) background gradient image usage/fallback, (2) headline color/typography/placement, (3) button color/typography/size, (4) mandatory asymmetric button border-radius. These have different hardness levels and exception conditions and must not be left unclustered or merged into a single generic rule.

payload.note: create separate rules for background/fallback, headline, button style, and border-radius

findings.description: The button radius rule is explicitly labeled 'mandatory, never change' and 'always required on every instance' — this is a hard/never-deviate rule with an explicit no-exception clause ('never simplify it to a single value, swap the corners, or round the decimals'). No governance facet (hardness=mandatory, no-exceptions) has been captured for this since no rule row exists at all.

The background-skip exception is conditional and scoped narrowly ('only in very, very rare cases': plain-text/AMP fallback, image-stripping clients, hard size budget). This scoping/condition governance is not captured anywhere since no rule rows exist; without it the fallback could be misapplied broadly.

payload.scope: rare fallback contexts only (plain-text/AMP, image-stripping clients, size budget)

findings.description: Missed relation: the border-radius mandatory rule explicitly states it applies 'even when the background is skipped', directly linking it to the background-fallback rule. This cross-rule dependency (radius rule overrides/persists through the fallback condition) is not represented since no rules exist yet.

The 'do not re-host or recolor' instruction for the background asset is a distinct hard prohibition (negative polarity) separate from the sizing/positioning rules, and should not be merged into a single generic background rule without capturing its own polarity/hardness.

payload.value: do not re-host or recolor background asset
